reasoning: The Lean statement asserts exactly the informal claim. The inner `⋃ (a : ℚ) (b : ℚ) (_ : a < b), {Set.Ioo (a : ℝ) b}` is precisely the set of open intervals (a,b) ⊂ ℝ with rational endpoints a < b. Applying `Set.image2 (fun s t => s ×ˢ t)` to two such collections yields exactly the collection {(a,b) × (c,d) : a, b, c, d ∈ ℚ, a < b, c < d}. The ambient space is ℝ × ℝ (inferred), which has the standard product topology, matching ℝ². Mathlib's `IsTopologicalBasis` is the standard Munkres-style notion of a basis (covers the space, inter-condition, generates the topology). The descriptor "countable" in the informal statement is just a description of the collection (automatically true here) rather than a separate claim. No hypotheses or conclusions drift.
